events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 1 October 2021, the Ukrainian Forces fired grenade launchers at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Sokilnyky, Luhansk. There were no injuries or fatalities.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 14 November 2021, the NAF rebel forces fired 152mm mortars at the governmental positions near Orikhove-Donetske, Luhansk. During the day, one military driver was wounded at an unspecified location.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 11 January 2022, the Ukrainian Forces fired heavy machine guns at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Kalynivka, Donetsk. There were no injuries or fatalities.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 22 February 2022, the Ukrainian forces fired 122mm and 82mm mortars at the positions of Donbass People's Militia near Zalizna Balka, Donetsk. During the day, twenty-seven locations were targeted. As a result of shelling, three civilians were killed and four DPR rebels were wounded at unspecified locations (fatalities coded to Donetsk - Kyivskyi event). The OSCE SMM observed a total of 8 explosions involving unknown weapons near Zalizna Balka.
On 29 March 2022, Ukrainian forces fired mortars at DPR forces in the direction of Donetsk - Donetsk Airport. Casualties unknown.
On 20 April 2022, Russian forces shelled Novodruzhesk, Luhansk, hitting a gas line. There were no casualties.
On 21 April 2022, Ukrainian forces clashed with Russian forces near Kurakhove, Donetsk. Around 250 Russian soldiers were killed.
On 9 May 2022, Ukrainian forces fired 82mm mortars at DPR positions in Holmivskyi, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 10 May 2022, there was an armed clash between Russian and Ukrainian forces in Bilohorivka, Luhansk. According to Ukrainian sources, about 80 Russian soldiers were killed.

On 11 May 2022, a Russian tank regiment attempted to cross the Siverskyi Donets River near Bilohorivka, Luhansk region. Ukrainian forces destroyed more than 80 units of Russian military equipment and killed 485 Russian servicemen.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 23 May 2022, Russian air forces struck the Ukrainian military near Minkivka, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 6 June 2022, following heavy clashes, Russian and DPR forces took control over Petrivske, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 14 June 2022, during the day, Ukrainian forces killed 73 Russian soldiers and destroyed about 20 Russian military vehicles in Donbas area (coded to Donetsk).

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 19 June 2022, Ukrainian forces fired 122mm artillery at DPR positions in the direction of Zaitseve, Donetsk. Russian forces shelled Ukrainian artillery systems. Civilian infrastructure was damaged. 1 civilian was killed.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 29 June 2022, Russian air forces struck a Ukrainian artillery platoon near Avdiivka, Donetsk. Additionally, the Russian military shelled positions of Ukrainian forces near Avdiivka, killing and wounding, according to Russian sources, half a brigade near Avdiivka (half a brigade estimated as 500 people, 250 coded as fatalities).

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 1 July 2022, Russian air and land forces carried out air strike and fired artillery at Ukrainian military forces and a command point in Bakhmut, Donetsk. According to Russian sources, up to 120 Ukrainian soldiers were killed, military equipment destroyed. Ukrainian sources reported injures of 5 civilians.
On 1 July 2022, Russian forces shelled near Loskutivka, Luhansk. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 7 July 2022, Russian air forces struck a hotel and residential high-rise in the center of Kramatorsk, Donetsk, killing 1 and wounding 6 civilians. Russian sources claimed to destroyed 2 Ukrainian 'Tochka-U' artillery systems and kill more than 150 Ukrainian soldiers (overall civilian and military fatalities coded as 151).
On 14 July 2022, Russian forces shelled near Krasnopillia, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 25 July 2022, Ukrainian forces fired 122mm missiles at DPR positions in Donetsk - Petrovskyi, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 5 August 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled DPR in Olenovka, Donetsk with 155mm artillery. Casualties unknown.

On 6 August 2022, Russian military forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Bakhmut, Donetsk, using artillery and carrying out air strikes but were repelled by Ukrainian forces. According to Russian sources, up to 130 Russian soldiers were killed and 8 military vehicles destroyed.

On 14 August 2022, Ukrainian forces launched a missile attack (likely M142 HIMARS) and hit PMC Wagner HQ in Popasna (Luhansk) inflicting heavy losses among mercenaries. According to Luhansk Military Administration, more than 100 mercenaries were killed.
On 17 August 2022, Russian forces shelled near Slovyansk, Donetsk region. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 28 August 2022, DPR forces supported by Military forces of Russia clashed with Ukrainian forces near Zaitseve, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

Around 5 September 2022 (week of), Ukrainian forces shelled Kreminna, Luhansk. As a result, about 400 Russian soldiers were killed in a few days. Ukrainian forces also destroys about 100 armored vehicles.
On 10 September 2022, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Soledar, Donetsk, using artillery but were repelled by Ukrainian forces. Casualties unknown.
On 22 September 2022, following clashes, Ukrainian forces recaptured Rubtsi, Donetsk region. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 2 October 2022, Russian forces launched air strikes and shelled Ukrainian positions in Yampolivka, Donetsk region. According to Russian sources, at least 200 Ukrainian soldiers were killed and 320 wounded, 14 tanks and 25 IFVs were destroyed.

On 4 October 2022, Russian forces shelled Ukrainian positions near Yakovlivka, Donetsk. Up to 70 Ukrainian soldiers were killed across Bilohorivka, Spirne and Yakovlivka. One tank, 3 armored fighter vehicles, and 6 pick-up trucks with heavy machine guns were also destroyed. [Russian MoD reported 70 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event]

On 18 October 2022, Ukrainian forces repelled Russian attacks near Bakhmutske, Donetsk region. Additionally, Russian forces shelled near Bakhmutske with tanks, mortars and artillery. Casualties unknown.
On 28 October 2022, Ukrainian forces conducted a high-precision missile strike near Maiorsk, Donetsk region as a result of which around 300 Russian servicemen were killed.
On 2 November 2022, Russian forces shelled Chasiv Yar, Donetsk region. Casualties unknown.

On 7 November 2022, Russian forces shelled, assaulted, and were repulsed from Ukrainian positions near Pavlivka, Donetsk. Ukrainian forces also shelled the positions of Russian forces, destroying 2 T-72B3 tanks and 3 infantry fighting vehicles BMP-2, and killed around 300 Russian soldiers.
On 10 November 2022, Ukrainian military forces killed 220 Russian soldiers during fighting on the eastern direction (coded to Donetsk, Donetsk).
On 16 November 2022, Russian forces shelled near New York, Donetsk region, with tanks and different types of artillery. Casualties unknown.
On 30 November 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled DPR in Horlivka, Donetsk region. 1 civilian was injured.
On 14 December 2022, Russian military forces shelled in the area of Spirne, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 27 December 2022, Russian forces shelled near Vuhledar, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 1 January 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Bakhmut, Donetsk, but were repelled by Ukrainian forces. Around 170 Russian servicemen were killed and more than 200 Russian servicemen were injured.

On 1 January 2023, Ukrainian forces launched 6 'HIMARS' missiles at Russian and DPR positions near Makiivka, Donetsk. Russian forces shot down 2 Ukrainian ' HIMARS' missiles. Civilian infrastructure was damaged. Ukraine claims that around 600 Russian servicemen were killed. Russia claims that 89 were killed and hundreds were wounded.
On 10 January 2023, Russian forces shelled near Pervomayske, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
Around 12 January 2023 (as reported), Ukrainian military forces shelled Russian forces in the area of Soledar, Donetsk. About 100 Russian soldiers were killed.

On 14 January 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Soledar, Donetsk, but were repelled by Ukrainian forces. 115 Russian servicemen were killed and 63 Russian servicemen were injured.

On 17 January 2023, as the result of an armed clash with Ukrainian forces, Russian forces took control over Sil, Donetsk. According to the Russian Ministry of Defence, up to 90 Ukrainian servicemen, three armored combat vehicles, four vehicles, an Msta-B howitzer and a Grad MLRS combat vehicle were destroyed in this direction.
On 24 January 2023, Russian forces shelled near Chervonopopivka, Luhansk. Casualties unknown.
On 26 January 2023, Ukrainian military forces repelled a Russian attack in the area of Bakhmut, Donetsk. According to Ukrainian sources, 600-700 Russian servicemen were killed during the day.

On 28 January 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Vuhledar, Donetsk, but were repelled by Ukrainian forces. A Ukrainian ammunition depot was destroyed. Up to 100 Ukrainian servicemen were killed near Vuhledar, Prechystivka, Shevchenko and Dorozhnianka. Additionally, 108 Russian servicemen were killed and 136 Russian servicemen were injured. [Russian MoD reported 100 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 4 events. 2 fatalities coded to this event]. Overall fatalities coded as 110.

On 1 February 2023, Russian and Wagner forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled with tanks and artillery near Bakhmut, Donetsk region. Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attack, however, Wagner forces advanced along certain streets on the northern and eastern outskirts of Bakhmut. Ukrainian forces also shelled Russian positions near Bakhmut. On that day, a total of 127 Russian soldiers were killed and 137 were wounded across multiple locations on the Bakhmut axis.
On 5 February 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Verkhnokamianske, Donetsk, and were repelled by Ukrainian forces. Casualties unknown.

On 8 February 2023, Russian forces shelled and clashed with Ukrainian forces near Bakhmut, Donetsk region. At least 7 clashes occurred during the day. Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attacks. On that day, about 128 Russian soldiers were killed and 137 were injured in various locations along Bakhmut axis (coded to Bakhmut, Donetsk region).

On 15 February 2023, Russian and Wagner forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled near Bakhmut, Donetsk region, with mortars and artillery. Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attack. During the day, 21 armed clash took place near Bakhmut. 117 Russian soldiers were killed and 199 were injured.

On 16 February 2023, Russian ground and air forces attacked near Vuhledar, Donetsk, but were repelled by the Ukrainian military. The Russian military destroyed a Ukrainian military warehouse. About 70 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in the Vuhledar direction.
On 16 February 2023, Ukrainian military forces shelled DPR's forces in Mineralne, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

Around 18 February 2023, following an armed clash with the Ukrainian military, Russian military forces claimed Paraskoviivka, Donetsk. More than 115 Ukrainian servicemen were killed. Ministry of Defence of Russia confirmed the event on 19 February.

On 18 February 2023, armed clashes between Russian and Ukrainian forces took place on the outskirts of Vuhledar, Donetsk region. Russian forces fired artillery at Ukrainian positions near Vuhledar, Donetsk. Russian sources claim that up to 70 Ukrainian servicemen were killed.
On 23 February 2023, Russian military forces attacked near Kreminna, Luhansk, but were repelled by the Ukrainian military. Russian military forces lost about 70 soldiers.

On 27 February 2023, Russian forces shelled near Shevchenko, Donetsk. Russia claims that the Ukrainian losses in this direction included two infantry fighting vehicles and three pickup trucks. Russia claims that more than 45 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in Vuhledar, Shevchenko and Zelenyi Hai. [Russian MoD reported 45 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 4 fatalities coded to this event]

On 8 March 2023, Russian and Wagner forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled near Bakhmut, Donetsk region. Ukrainian forces repelled some of the Russian attacks while also withdrew some of its units from the city. 19 clashes took place near Bakhmut, 127 Russian soldiers were killed and 135 were injured on the Bakhmut axis during that day.
On 10 March 2023, Russian forces shelled near Kuzmine, Luhansk. Casualties unknown.

On 11 March 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Bakhmut, Donetsk, and were repelled by Ukrainian forces. 221 Russian servicemen were killed, 314 were wounded.

On 18 March 2023, Russian forces engaged in an unsuccessful offensive in the direction of Bakhmut, Donetsk, and were repelled by Ukrainian forces. Wagner forces captured unspecified industrial facilities in the AZOM complex in northern Bakhmut. Ukrainian forces shelled Russian positions and an ammunition depot near Bakhmut. 193 Russian servicemen were killed and 199 Russian servicemen were wounded. 2 civilians were injured.

On 20 March 2023, Russian forces shelled near Vuhledar, Donetsk. Russia claims that the Ukrainian losses in this direction included two pickup trucks, and a D-30 howitzer. Russia claims that up to 30 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in Vuhledar, Novomykhaylivka and Preobrazhenka. [Russian MoD reported 30 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event]

On 22 March 2023, Russian forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled near Avdiivka, Donetsk region. During the day, Russian forces made 26 assaults on Avdiivka axis, Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attacks. Ukrainian sources claim that on that day, at least 100 Russian soldiers were killed, 240 were injured, 2 tanks, 2 armored carriers, 4 IFVs, 2 radiolocation combat systems and artillery systems were destroyed.

On 28 March 2023, Russian forces shelled, assaulted, and were repulsed from Ukrainian positions near Bakhmut, Donetsk. Russian forces advanced in southern Bakhmut and up to the Bakhmut City Market in central Bakhmut. During the day, Ukrainian forces destroyed the hideouts of Wagner group and killed around 86 soldiers. 117 were wounded.

On 29 March 2023, Russian forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled near Bakhmut, Donetsk region. Wagner fighters advanced further in the southern and western parts of Bakhmut. In total, Ukrainian forces repelled 28 attacks during the day. Russian forces have made some territorial gains; the fighting seems to take place in the city center. On that day, Ukrainian forces also shot down a Russian Su-24 aircraft near Bakhmut. According to Ukrainian sources, 97 Russian soldiers were killed and 137 were wounded on that day near Bakhmut.
On 30 March 2023, Russian military forces, artillery and air forces attacked the Ukrainian military near Dibrova, Luhansk. On Dibrova direction, up to 85 Ukrainian soldiers were killed.
On 31 March 2023, Russian forces shelled Ukrainian military positions in Chasiv Yar, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 5 April 2023, Russian forces assaulted, clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled near Bakhmut, Donetsk region, seeking to gain control over the entire territory of the city. Ukrainian forces shelled Russian infantry around Bakhmut. In total, on that day there were 21 armed clashes near Bakhmut, Bohdanivka and Ivanivske. Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attack. On that day, according to Ukrainian sources, Ukrainian forces killed 88 and wounded 124 Russian soldiers, destroyed 3 UAVs, 3 field ammo depots and one radioelectronic combat system.

On 10 April 2023, Russian forces and Wagner group clashed with Ukrainian forces near Bakhmut, Donetsk. Russian forces made advances in northwestern and central Bakhmut. As a result of the shelling, 1 civilian was injured. During the day, Russian missile and artillery forces carried out 48 firing missions. The Ukrainian losses during the day amounted to more than 450 Ukrainian servicemen, including foreign fighters from the international legion, three tanks, three infantry fighting vehicles, six armored fighting vehicles, six vehicles, D-20 and MSTA-B howitzers, as well as two AN/TPQ-50 counter-battery radar stations.

On 10 April 2023, Russian and Ukrainian forces clashed in the area of the Zhuravka gully near Yampolivka, Donetsk region, as a result of which Russian forces occupied Ukrainian strongholds there. Russian forces shelled the positions of Ukrainian forces near Yampolivka. During the day, Ukrainian losses in this direction amounted to more than 90 Ukrainian soldiers, three armored fighting vehicles, a pickup truck, and a D-30 howitzer.

On 12 April 2023, Russian forces engaged in 22 clashes with Ukrainian forces near Bakhmut, Bohdanivka and Khromove, launched 5 air strikes and shelled 225 times near Bakhmut, Donetsk region, striving to establish full control over the city. Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian attack. Wagner forces fired artillery at Ukrainian positions in Bakhmut. According to Ukrainian sources, 126 Russian soldiers were killed and 184 were wounded, 6 ammo depots, an IFV and a radiolocation system were destroyed.

On 13 April 2023, Wagner group and Russian paratroopers attacked the Ukrainian military, including the State Border Guard Service, in Bakhmut, Donetsk. Ukrainian artillery also destroyed a Russian military warehouse nearby. Overall, 84 Russian soldiers were killed, 92 wounded.
On 23 April 2023, Russian forces shelled Pivdenne, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 25 April 2023, Russian forces shelled, assaulted, and were repulsed from Ukrainian positions in Bakhmut, Donetsk. During the day Ukrainian forces killed 175 Russian servicemen and destroyed four Russian ammunition depots near Bakhmut. Russia claims that the Ukrainian losses in this direction included two infantry fighting vehicles, four armored fighting vehicles, seven vehicles, D-20 and Msta-B howitzers, an Akatsiya self-propelled artillery unit, as well as one L118 howitzer. Russia claims that more than 300 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in Bakhmut, Bohdanivka and Chervone. [Russian MoD reported 300 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event]. Overall fatalities coded as 178.

On 26 April 2023, Russian and Wagner forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and shelled in Bakhmut, Donetsk region. Russian troops advanced to Persha Lisova Street in western Bakhmut and Wagner forces reached the intersection of Tchaikovskyi and Yuvileina Streets. During the day, there were 13 armed clashes, Russian forces shelled 243 times in various locations on Bakhmut axis (coded to Bakhmut, Donetsk region). According to Ukrainian sources, 113 Russian soldiers were killed and 119 were injured, a cannon, 4 drones, 4 field ammo depots and military vehicles were destroyed.
On 29 April 2023, Russian forces launched missiles at Ukrainian positions in Kostiantynivka, Donetsk. More than 200 Ukrainian servicemen were killed and around 280 Ukrainian servicemen were injured.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 1 October 2021, the Ukrainian Forces fired grenade launchers and small arms at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Kalinovo, Luhansk. There were no injuries or fatalities.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 20 October 2021, the Ukrainian Forces fired grenade launchers and small arms at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Holubivske, Luhansk. There were no injuries or fatalities.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 7 November 2021, the Ukrainian Forces fired grenade launchers at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Kalinovo, Luhansk. There were no casualties.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 17 November 2021, the Ukrainian Forces fired 122mm and 120mm mortars at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Sentianivka, Luhansk. There were no casualties. The OSCE SMM observed a total of 1 bursts of fire involving heavy machineguns near Sentianivka.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 29 November 2021, the NAF rebel forces fired 120mm mortars at the governmental positions near Staryi Aidar, Luhansk. During the day, two Ukrainian soldiers were wounded at unspecified locations.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 23 December 2021, the OSCE SMM observed a total of 2 projectiles involving unknown weapons near Bohuslavske.
On 23 January 2022, the OSCE SMM observed a total of 36 explosions involving unknown weapons near Molodizhne.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 14 February 2022, the OSCE SMM observed a total of 2 explosions involving unknown weapons near Schastia.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 18 February 2022, the OSCE SMM observed a total of 16 explosions involving unknown weapons near Bohuslavske.
On 20 February 2022, the OSCE SMM observed a total of 53 explosions involving unknown weapons near Pamyatnik knyazyu Igoryu.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 22 February 2022, the Ukrainian Forces fired grenade launchers at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Obozne, Luhansk. There were no injuries or fatalities. The OSCE SMM observed a total of 20 explosions involving unknown weapons near Obozne.

On 24 February 2022, the military forces of Russia launched a rocket attack on Schastia, Luhansk. Fighting for the city continued throughout the day, as a result of which the Ukrainian military forces regained control over Schastia and eliminated 50 soldiers of Russian military forces.

On 25 February 2022, the Russian military forces fired unspecified weapons at Ukrainian government positions in Starobilsk, Luhansk. As a result of the shelling, two residential houses and gas line were damaged.
On 7 March 2022, Ukrainian military forces destroyed a column of Russian military forces during a clash in Luhansk region, killing around 40 Russian soldiers.
On 14 March 2022, Ukrainian forces fired 120mm mortars at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia near Pervomaisk, Luhansk. Casualties unknown.

On 7 April 2022, clashes took place between the Russian and Ukrainian forces in the Luhansk region as a result of which Ukrainian forces captured the position of the Russian forces as well as Russian military equipment. At least 3 Russian soldiers were wounded. LPR claims that Russian forces killed 34 Ukrainian soldiers and destroyed 7 armored personnel carriers and 12 vehicles in the Luhansk region. [coded to Luhansk as the exact location is unknown]
On 11 April 2022, Ukrainian military fired 122mm mortars at LPR positions near Pervomaisk, Luhansk. As a result of the shelling, four residential buildings were damaged. Casualties unknown.

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 18 April 2022, Ukrainian military fired 152mm and 122mm mortars at LPR positions near Pervomaisk, Luhansk. Ukrainian forces also shelled Pervomiask using BM-30 Smerch. According to the sources of LPR, about twenty-eight Ukrainian soldiers were killed. LPR forces claimed destruction of two tanks, two infantry fighting vehicles, one armored personnel carrier, three vehicles of Ukrainian forces. As a result of the fighting, a few residential buildings, a school, children's sports complex, a bank, a grocery store, and an administration of the town were damaged.

On 28 April 2022, Russian forces destroyed 3 armored personnel carriers and 5 vehicles in the Luhansk region (coded to Luhansk) belonging to Ukrainian forces. Additionally, 15 Ukrainian servicemen were killed throughout the day.

On 3 May 2022, Ukrainian forces fired BM-21 Grad missiles, 152mm and 122mm mortars at the positions of the Luhansk People's Militia in Pervomaisk, Luhansk. As a result of the shelling, two civilians were wounded and two residential buildings were destroyed.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 19 May 2022, LPR forces destroyed 4 armored personnel carriers and 5 vehicles belonging to the Ukrainian forces as well as killed 17 Ukrainian servicemen in the Luhansk region (coded to Luhansk).

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 26 May 2022, during clashes with Ukrainian forces, LPR forces destroyed 2 tanks, 4 armored personnel carriers and 7 vehicles belonging to the Ukrainian forces as well as killed 24 Ukrainian servicemen in the Luhansk region.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 2 June 2022, LPR forces destroyed 5 armored personnel carriers and 8 vehicles belonging to the Ukrainian forces as well as killed 33 Ukrainian servicemen during clashes in the Luhansk region.
On 3 June 2022, Ukrainian forces fired artillery and Grad missiles at Pervomaisk, Luhansk. 2 civilians were injured.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) Air Force
events.notes: On 9 June 2022, Ukrainian forces launched an airstrike on the Wagner group military base in Kadiivka (Stakhanov), Luhansk. According to the Luhansk military administration, all Wagner group members, who were inside the base, were killed, except for one person. On 10 June, according to Russian sources, the number of (unspecified) killed reached 22.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya

russia_ukraine.name: Russia ↔ Ukraine
russia_ukraine.summary: Full-scale Russian invasion since Feb 2022. Documented BGP rerouting of occupied Ukrainian territory through Russian state-operated transit subject to SORM legal-intercept.

events.actor1: Police Forces of Ukraine (2019-) Security Service of Ukraine
events.notes: Weapons seizure: On 18 October 2021, the Security Service of Ukraine found and seized a cache of weapons in Kherson, consisting of two boxes of rifle ammunition and two hand grenades.
On 1 April 2022, Russian forces abducted a chief priest in Kherson. He was later released during the day.

On 1 May 2022, during occupation, Russian forces and a Federal Security Service officer abducted a 37-year-old Ukrainian man in Lazurne, Kherson. They put a bag over his head and took him to one of the captured sanatoriums. According to the investigation, the Russian 'deputy commandant' and his subordinates interrogated the detainee, demanding information about his last place of work, possession of weapons, and participation in anti-occupation rallies. The man was beaten, and then continued to be abused over the next two days in order to force him to cooperate. Nevertheless, he was later released. The Ukrainian Regional Prosecutor's Officer identified 1 Russian military serviceman and a Russian Federal Security Service officer (FSB) for committing a war crime the former was sentenced to 12 years in prison in absentia (but will be tried for new episodes) and the FSB officer still faces criminal proceedings.

On 2 May 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) killed 122 Russian soldiers in their area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson). Also, a Russian recon and sabotage group made an attempt to capture a Ukrainian observation post, but were repelled by Ukrainian mortar fire and retreated to their original positions in the Kherson region.

On 7 May 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed at least 10 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 3 MLRS, 2 APCs, 2 units of automotive equipment, 1 damaged radar artillery reconnaissance complex 'Zoopark 2'), and killed at least 136 Russian soldiers in their area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson).

On 17 May 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed at least 26 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 1 airborne self-propelled artillery and mortar installation, 3 'T-72B3' modernized tanks, 10 armored vehicles, 12 cars/vehicles) and killed 130 Russian soldiers in their area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson).
On 31 May 2022, Russian forces shelled Ukrainian military positions in Novovorontsovka, Kherson. Casualties unknown.

On 5 June 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed at least 40 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., around 5 tanks, 4 'Msta-B' large-caliber artillery, 7 towed-artillery, 9 armored vehicles, 1 'Tor-M1' air defense missile system, 1 '2S1' self-propelled howitzer, 2 (Grad and Uragan) MLRS and 9 cars/vehicles), 3 field ammo depots and killed at least 146 Russian soldiers in their area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson).

On 26 June 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed 11 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 1 'T-72' tank, 4 armored vehicles, 3 cars/vehicles, 3 'Orlan-10' recon drones), 1 field ammo depot and killed 39 Russian soldiers in their area of operation. Russian air forces launched rockets at Ukrainian positions in Kherson region (coded to Kherson, Kherson).
On 11 July 2022, Ukrainian forces attacked Russian forces in Tavriyske, Kherson. According to Ukrainian sources, around 120 Russian soldiers were killed and around 200 were injured.
On 16 July 2022, Russian forces shelled near Trudolyubivka, Kherson. Casualties unknown.
On 31 July 2022, Russian forces fired artillery and MLRS near Blahodatne, Kherson. Casualties unknown.
On 11 August 2022, Russian forces shelled Tverdomedove, Kherson, with tanks and artillery. Casualties unknown.

events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) Air Force
events.notes: Interception: On 23 August 2022, Russian military forces intercepted 7 Ukrainian drones across 5 locations, some near Chornyanka, Kherson.

On 31 August 2022, Ukrainian military forces clashed with Russian forces, shelled and launched air strikes in Beryslav district (coded to Beryslav, Kherson region). Ukrainian forces destroyed two command strongpoints and a number of vehicles. The Russian military intercepted 48 Ukrainian MLRS missiles across 9 locations, some nearby. During the day, Ukrainian forces killed 201 Russian soldiers during armed clashes in the Kherson region (201 fatality split between 2 events).
On 8 September 2022, Russian forces shelled Tavriiske, Kherson. Casualties unknown.
On 10 September 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled a concentration of Russian forces in Kherson City as a result of which over 180 Russian servicemen were killed.
On 16 September 2022, Russian forces shelled Myroliubivka, Kherson. Casualties unknown.

On 24 September 2022, Russian forces shelled Ukrainian positions in Vysokopillia, Kherson. More than 110 Ukrainian soldiers were killed across the Mykolaiv-Kryvyi Rih direction of fighting, with 30 killed near Bezimenne, Myroliubivka and Osokorivka (remaining 80 fatalities split between Lozove, Ochakiv, Ternovi Pody, Kalynivka, Ukrainka, Myrne, Shyroke, Blahodativka, Mykolaiv, Vysokopillia, Posad Pokrovske, Sukhyi Stavok, Arkhanhelske, Olhyne and Lymany). 13 military vehicles were destroyed. [Russian MoD reported 80 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 15 events. 1 fatality coded to this event]
On 4 October 2022, Russian forces shelled the residential area in Novovorontsovka, Kherson. There were some killed and wounded local civilians. Fatalities coded as 0.

On 6 October 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed 47 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 18 'Msta-B' large-caliber artillery and 'Msta-S' self-propelled howitzers, 1 (152mm) towed-artillery, 8 tanks, 15 cars/armored-vehicles, 1 '2S7' self-propelled howitzer, 4 mortars) and killed 105 Russian soldiers in their area of operation. Also, 6 more units of military hardware, including IFV, artillery and radio-electronic station were damaged (coded to Kherson city, Kherson region).

On 7 October 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed 28 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 8 'Msta-B' towed-artillery and 'Msta-S' self-propelled howitzers, 6 tanks, 9 cars/armored-vehicles, 1 'BM-21' MLRS, 2 'S-300' launcher pads, 1 '2S9' self-propelled mortar, 1 'Sani' mortar system), 1 stronghold and killed 110 Russian soldiers in their area of operation. Also, 9 more units of military hardware, including a tank, artillery and several cars/armored-vehicles were damaged (coded to Kherson city, Kherson region).

On 10 October 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South', using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed 18 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 2 'Msta-B' towed-artillery, 12 car/armored-vehicles, 2 tanks, 2 mortars), 2 field ammo depots and killed 174 Russian soldiers in their area of operation. Also, 5 more units of military hardware, including 1 'Giatsynt-B' large-caliber artillery were damaged (coded to Kherson city, Kherson region).
On 12 October 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian positions near Tokarivka, Kherson region, killing up to 150 Russian soldiers.

On 12 October 2022, Russian forces repelled an attack of Ukrainian forces in the direction of Pravdyne, Kherson region. At least 110 Ukrainian soldiers were killed near Pyatykhatky, Kostromka, Bezvodne and Pravdyne, and 21 armored fighter vehicles and 12 special cars were destroyed. Casualties and material losses on Russian side unknown. [Russian MoD reported 110 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 4 events. 2 fatalities coded to this event]

On 21 October 2022, Russian forces clashed with Ukrainian forces and prevented Ukrainian counteroffensive on the Davydiv Brid - Sukhyi Stavok area in Kherson region. Russian forces claim that on that day, at least 120 Ukrainian soldiers were killed, 6 IFVs and 13 cars were destroyed in Davydiv Brid - Sukhyi Stavok area.

On 21 October 2022, Ukrainian forces clashed with Russian forces near Pyatykhatky, Kherson region. Russian forces also shelled Ukrainian positions near Pyatykhatky. Russian sources claim that around that day, at least 130 Ukrainian soldiers were killed, 2 tanks, 9 IFVs, 13 cars were destroyed during ground operations near Sukhanove, Pyatykhatky, Pravdyne, Bezvodne, Sablukivka and Bruskynske. [Russian MoD reported 130 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 6 events. 2 fatalities coded to this event]

Around 1 November 2022 (week of), Russian forces shot and arrested a person in Kherson, Kherson region, who was then brought to Kherson pre-trial detention center, later transferred to Kherson Colony No. 61 and tortured to 'confess' of being an artillery spotter.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) Air Force
events.notes: On 9 November 2022, Ukrainian forces launched 8 air strikes and shelled Russian positions in Kherson area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson region). At least 125 Russian soldiers were killed during the day in Kherson area of operation. 3 tanks and 5 armored carriers were destroyed.

On 9 November 2022, Russian forces blew up a crossing on the Dnieper near Sadove, Kherson region, while retreating from the areas of Kherson region on the right bank of the Dnieper to slow down the advancement of Ukrainian troops.

On 10 November 2022, during clashes throughout the day, Ukrainian forces (of Operational Command 'South',using aviation, UAVs, missiles and artillery) destroyed 8 units of Russian military equipment (i.e., 3 tanks, 8 cars/armored-vehicles), 1 field ammo depot and killed 125 Russian soldiers in their area of operation (coded to Kherson, Kherson). According to Russian sources, Ukrainian forces launched 28 missiles from reactive missile systems in the region of Kherson, Kherson. 5 missiles were intercepted.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 19 November 2022, Russian air and ground forces fired missiles and artillery at Ukrainian positions near Vesele, Kherson. Casualties unknown.

On 30 November 2022, Ukrainian forces carried out a missile strike at the concentration of Russian military personnel and their military hardware in Russian-held Skadovsk, Kherson. Casualties unknown.
Interception: On 16 December 2022, Ukrainian forces shot down two Russian missiles in Kherson oblast (coded to Kherson, Kherson.)

On 20 December 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian field airfield near Kakhovka, Kherson. Russians lost up to 150 people killed and about 50 wounded. Up to 20 units of military equipment of various types were destroyed.
On 29 December 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian forces near Fedorivka, Kherson killing approximately 200 Russian soldiers.
On 31 December 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian force concentration in Chulakivka, Kherson region. Ukrainian forces claim that 500 Russian soldiers were killed and wounded.
On 1 January 2023, Russian forces shelled Respublikanets, Kherson. Civilian infrastructure was damaged. Casualties unknown.
On 18 January 2023, a civilian was injured near Borozenske, Kherson region, having accidentally activated an explosive while he was doing agricultural works.
On 29 January 2023, Russian forces shelled Oleshky, Kherson region, damaging the House of Culture. Casualties unknown.

Security measures: Around 8 February 2023 (week of), Russian authorities imposed filtration activities in Novotroitske, Kherson region, especially against those civilians who were employed with Ukrainian authorities and refused to collaborate with the Russian forces.
On 20 February 2023, Russian forces shelled near Ivanivka, Kherson. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 2 March 2023, a Russian drone struck Ukrainian civilians who waited for humanitarian aid in Kozatske, Kherson. 9 civilians were wounded.
On 13 March 2023, Russian forces shelled Zmiivka, Kherson. There were no casualties.
On 22 March 2023, Russian forces shelled Ukrainian military positions in Kozatske, Kherson. Casualties unknown.
On 5 April 2023, Russian forces shelled Havrylivka, Kherson region. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 20 April 2023, Russian air forces and artillery killed up to 10 Ukrainian soldiers and destroyed 2 cars in the Kherson direction (coded to Kherson, Kherson).
On 3 May 2023, Russian forces shelled Novokayiry, Kherson region. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: Around 15 May 2023 (month of), a Russian soldier dropped an explosive from a FPV drone at two residents of Odradokamianka, Kherson region. 1 civilian was injured.
On 30 May 2023, Russian forces shelled near Kizomys, Kherson, injuring 1 civilian.

As a result of shelling, two Ukrainian soldiers were killed and eighteen were wounded at unspecified locations (fatalities coded to Talakivka event).

events.notes: On 21 February 2022, the Ukrainian forces fired 122mm and 120mm mortars and grenade launchers at the positions of Donbass People's Militia near Zayichenko, Donetsk. During the day, twenty-eight locations were targeted. As a result of shelling, one DPR rebel was killed and three were wounded at unspecified locations (fatalities coded to Zalizna Balka event).

events.actor2: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 24 February 2022, the Ukrainian forces fired unspecified weapons at the positions of Donbass People's Militia near Kominternove, Donetsk. During the day, eight locations were targeted. As a result, one civilian was killed and eight were wounded (fatalities coded to Horlivka event).
On 26 February 2022, Russian military forces shelled Sartana, Donetsk. Three people were wounded and three killed, including a child and two Greek citizens.

On 27 February 2022, there was an armed clash between Russian military forces and Ukrainian military forces in Mariupol, Donetsk. Russian military forces also launched a missile into the residential complex in the city. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: Around 1 March 2022 (as reported), according to Russian sources, following clashes with Ukrainian military, 'DPR' forces established control over Talakivka, Donetsk. 2 civilians were killed as a result of fighting. [Russian WarGonzo reported 2 fatalities. Coded as 2 fatalities.]

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.notes: On 1 March 2022, according to Russian sources, following clashes with Ukrainian military, 'DPR' forces established control over Lebedynske, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 2 March 2022, Russian forces fired artillery at maternity hospital, several houses in Levoberezhny neighborhood, and a missile at 42th school in Kalmiusk district in Mariupol, Donetsk. Three civilians died.

On 6 March 2022, the Russian military forces clashed with Ukrainian forces near Mariupol, Donetsk. Ukrainian forces carried out a counterattack in Mariupol direction, destroyed 3 tanks, 4 infantry fighting vehicles, 2 armoured vehicles 'Tiger' and killed about 30 Russian soldiers. Two Russian soldiers were taken as war prisoners.

On 6 March 2022, the Russian military forces shelled the city of Mariupol, Donetsk. The evacuation of civilians from the city was cancelled. The entire areas of the city were destroyed and burned by the Russian occupants. Thousands of civilians were reported wounded. At least one child was killed.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 8 March 2022, Russian air force dropped bombs on Mir boulevard and Torgovaya street in Mariupol, Donetsk. At least 5 civilians, including 2 children, were killed.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 9 March 2022, Russian air force conducted air strikes at the 2nd maternity hospital and at the children's hospital in Mariupol. Three people, including a child, were killed, 17 wounded. Another pregnant woman and her child died later in a hospital.

Around 10 March 2022 (as reported), according to 'DPR' sources, following withdrawal from the settlement, Ukrainian forces heavily shelled Staryi Krym, Donetsk, with MLRS (Grad). As a result of shelling, 5 civilians were killed. Also, around 80 local civilians have been evacuated. [Russian WarGonzo reported 5 fatalities. Coded as 5 fatalities.]

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 10 March 2022, Russian forces throughout the whole day conducted airstrikes and shelled Mariupol (Donetsk) with heavy weapons as well as fired on the green corridor through which humanitarian aid was supposed to be transported to the city. One civilian died from an airstrike. Further casualties unknown. In another event, at night, Russian aerial bomb fell on a private house killing an 11-year-old girl (gymnast) and her father on the spot (fatalities coded as 3).

Around 10 March 2022 (as reported), according to 'DPR' and Russian sources, following withdrawal from the settlement, Ukrainian forces heavily shelled Manhush, Donetsk, with MLRS (Grad). As a result of shelling, 3 civilians were killed, including 2 children. [Russian WarGonzo reported 3 fatalities. Coded as 3 fatalities.]

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 11 March 2022, Russian forces conducted an airstrike and shelled Mariupol, Donetsk throughout the whole night, hitting a high-rise residential building in the city's Central District at Mytropolytska Street 98. As a result of the attack, around 17 civilians were killed, including children.

events.actor1: Police Forces of Ukraine (2019-) State Border Guard Service
events.notes: On 12 March 2022, there was an armed clash between Ukrainian Border Guard and Russian forces near Mariupol, Donetsk. As a result, 15 Russian soldiers were killed.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 13 March 2022, Russian forces throughout the whole day conducted airstrikes and shelled Mariupol, Donetsk, with heavy weapons. There was an armed clash between Russian forces and Ukrainian military forces, including Azov Battalion, as a result of which Azov Battalion destroyed three T-72 tanks and one armored vehicles 'Tiger'. Azov also captured 12 and killed 17 Russian soldiers.

On 14 March 2022, Russian forces tried to break through in the direction of Mariupol and clashed with Ukrainian forces, including Azov Battalion. Ukraine's artillery and aircraft inflicted fire damage on the accumulation of military equipment and enemy columns on the advance routes - 12 units of motor vehicles were destroyed as a result of the artillery fire. The 336th Separate Marine Brigade and the 11th Separate Assault Brigade also suffered losses. The total losses of Russian forces were about 150 people, 2 tanks, 7 infantry fighting vehicles and one armored personnel carrier, according to the information available.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: Around 15 March 2022 (as reported), a Russian airstrike killed around 100 civilians in Mariupol, Donetsk. The corpses were found at the end of June.

events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 15 March 2022, the Azov battalion while defending Mariupol clashed with Russian forces. A Russian general was killed, together with a part of the Russian 22nd Brigade. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10. Simultaneously, civilian infrastructure was severely damaged in Mariupol by Russian shelling, including a hospital.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 16 March 2022, Russian air force bombed the center of Mariupol, Donetsk. An airstrike destroyed a theatre in the city, which was officially designated as civilian infrastructure sheltering hundreds of civilians who were left without home. According to witness statements, around 300 people were killed. Another bomb hit a 'Neptune' swimming pool sheltering pregnant women and women with small children. At least one woman was wounded.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 17 March 2022, the Azov Battalion destroyed 4 tanks, 2 armored personnel carriers and a company of Russian infantry near Mariupol, Donetsk, inflicting fatalities. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10.
On 18 March 2022, Russian military forces shelled Mariupol, Donetsk. 4 civilians were killed, 1 wounded.

On 20 March 2022, Russian troops shelled Mariupol, Donetsk, from 4 navy ships of Russian Navy. Fight between Russian and Ukrainian forces for Azovstal steel plant continued. Ukrainian forces destroyed 4 Russian tanks. The deputy commander of Russia's Black Sea Fleet was killed in a battle, along with other soldiers. Street battles were raging in Mariupol. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10.

On 21 March 2022, an armed clash occurred between Russian and Ukrainian military forces in Mariupol, Donetsk. Russian forces also shelled Mariupol. Ukrainian forces destroyed the fastest boat in the Russian army. 28 Ukrainian soldiers were killed during the day in the Donetsk region. There were also fatalities and casualties on the Russian side, coded as additional 10 fatalities. [38 fatalities split among 10 events]

On 21 March 2022, one civilian man and two children were killed in an explosion near Manhush (Donetsk). They tried to evacuate on their own from the Russian held-territories, when their car hit a mine.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 22 March 2022, Ukrainian forces (Azov Battalion) clashed with Russian forces in street fights in Mariupol, Donetsk. At least 10 Russian soldiers were killed. Also, Ukrainian air defense shot down a Russian airplane in Mariupol.

Around 22 March 2022 (as reported), a Ukrainian marine fired an RPG on Russian 'T-72' tank, destroying it completely and killing its crew in Mariupol, Donetsk. Unspecified fatalities coded as 3. Also, Ukrainian marines shot down 1 Russian 'Orlan-10' drone in Mariupol.
On 23 March 2022, Russian forces fired artillery in the direction of the Ukrainian positions near Mariupol, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 24 March 2022, Russian forces shelled Mariupol, Donetsk, as a result of which 2 civilians died, including one child, and 14 were wounded.

On 25 March 2022, Russian forces shelled civilian buildings in Mariupol, Donetsk. Ukrainian forces, particularly Azov fighters and border guards, responded with fire, firing grenade launchers, destroying two BMD vehicles, five trucks, a tank, a howitzer, and damaging one armored vehicle. One civilian died, as well as around 20 Russian soldiers. 20 people were wounded.
Displacement: Around 27 March 2022, Russian forces forcibly deported children without parents from Mariupol to Donetsk.

On 28 March 2022, according to Ukrainian sources, in the morning, during the early days of Russian offensive on Mariupol, 3 women were standing near the entrance on the Budivelnyk Avenue in Mariupol city, Donetsk. They were waiting for the kettle to boil on the stove so they could drink some coffee when a Russian artillery shell landed nearby. All 3 women died instantly. A neighbor collected the remains of the deceased and took them to the pit on a wheelbarrow (six months later, they were reburied at the Starokrymskyi cemetery). One of the killed women worked at the port as a senior railway dispatcher, while two others were the pensioners.

events.actor1: NAF: United Armed Forces of Novorossiya
events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 31 March 2022, NAF forces shot down 2 Mil Mi-8 helicopters in the territory of Mariupol, Donetsk with alleged members of the Azov Battalion on board. As a result, 15 people died.
On 31 March 2022, as a result of shelling by Russian forces 2 civilians were killed near Melekyne village, Donetsk while attempting to escape from Mariupol.
On 31 March 2022, armed clashes continued between the Russian and Ukrainian forces around Mariupol, Donetsk. At least one Ukrainian serviceman died during the clashes.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 3 April 2022, Russian forces carried out air strikes at positions of the Ukrainian Army - Azov Battalion in Mariupol, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 8 April 2022, Russian forces and Donetsk rebel forces clashed with Ukrainian forces near the Azovstal plant in Mariupol. Azov battalion reports destroying an IFV, killing its personnel. Unspecified fatalities coded as 3. In the morning, Russian forces shelled the Kapitan Belousov icebreaker ship, killing one and injuring several people, and used naval rocket artillery to target the city. Other casualties unknown.

events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) National Guard (Azov)
events.notes: On 9 April 2022, Russian forces continued clashes with Azov Battalion trying to gain full control over Mariupol, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 11 April 2022, Russian military forces attacked Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk, but were repelled by the Ukrainian military, including Azov Battalion. The same day, 100 Ukrainian marines attempted to break out of the city, but were attacked by the Russian air forces and artillery. During the breakout, Ukrainian forces lost about 50 marines and 42 were taken as prisoners according to the Russian sources. [Russian MoD reported 50 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities]

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 11 April 2022, Russian forces using drones dropped a substance of unknown origin on Mariupol, Donetsk. It was used against Ukrainian military and civilians. As a result, victims suffered respiratory failure and vestibular balance disorder. There were no casualties. Additionally, Russian forces shelled Caritas Charitable Foundation building, killing 7 civilians.
Displacement: On 14 April 2022, 289 civilians evacuated from Mariupol, Donetsk, with their own cars to Zaporizhia.

On 15 April 2022, Russian forces launched air strikes, using Tu-22M3, SU-34, and SU-24M planes on Ukrainian forces in Mariupol, Donetsk. Ukrainian forces fired back on the ground in the port area, while the Russian forces gained control of the Ilyich factory. Two aid workers, and five other people, died.

On 19 April 2022, Russian forces continued bombing and shelling Mariupol, Donetsk. On the ground, their forces clashed with Ukrainian forces, including Azov Battalion, in the Azovstal plant. During the day, Russian forces dropped a bomb on a hospital near Azovstal. Casualties unknown.

On 24 April 2022, during the intense siege of Mariupol, Russian forces heavily shelled Mariupol city, Donetsk. A family of three attempted to take cover but the fallen debris killed a 14-year-old girl (she was a weightlifter athlete) and her mother instantly while her son who ran after them was shot dead by a Russian sniper.

Mass grave: Around 25 April 2022 (as reported), a mass grave of people killed by Russian forces was found through satellite photos in Staryi Krym, Donetsk. The number of people buried there is unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 28 April 2022, Russian forces carried out more than 50 airstrikes on the Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk where a large number of Ukrainian servicemen and civilians are located. Due to the attack, parts of the building collapsed, especially the operation room. There were dead and wounded soldiers and civilians. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10.

On 29 April 2022, Russian forces continued fighting with Ukrainian forces, including the National Guard, and conducting air strikes in Mariupol, Donetsk. National Guard soldiers fired mortars back at Russian forces. There were at least two fatalities.
Displacement: On 1 May 2022, civilians were evacuated from the Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk region.

On 2 May 2022, Russian forces launched an airstrike and artillery attack on Mariupol, Donetsk, and tried to storm Azovstal plant, inciting clashes with Ukrainian forces, including Azov Battalion, forces of 36th separate brigade of marines, units of the Military Forces, the National Guard, Border Guards and policemen. As a result, five Russian soldiers were killed. Further casualties unknown.

On 3 May 2022, Russian forces who broke into Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk, clashed with Ukrainian forces, including Azov Battalion, forces of 36th separate brigade of marines, units of the Military Forces, the National Guard, Border Guards and policemen. One policeman and two civilians were killed. Further casualties unknown.

On 5 May 2022, Russian forces continued conducting airstrikes and shelling the Azovstal factory in Mariupol, Donetsk region. Additionally, a day prior, some of Russian forces managed to get to the territory of the factory therefore continuous armed clashes are taking place between the Russian and Ukrainian forces. At least 6 Russian servicemen were killed.

On 6 May 2022, Russian forces, including the air force, clashed with Ukrainian forces, including the Azov Battalion forces in Mariupol, Donetsk. During an attempted evacuation, Russian forces attacked Ukrainian troops killing 3 and injuring 6 soldiers. Further casualties are unknown.
Displacement: On 6 May 2022, 50 civilians were evacuated from the Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk, to Ukraine-controlled territories.

On 13 May 2022, Russian forces, including air forces, clashed with Ukrainian forces, including the Azov Battalion forces, in the Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk. Russian side suffered losses. Unspecified fatalities coded as 10.
Around 15 May 2022 (month of), Russian forces killed at least 200 civilians in Mariupol, Donetsk. The corpses were found in the basement under the rubble of one of the apartment buildings.

On 15 May 2022, Russian forces carried out an airstrike and an artillery attack on Mariupol, Donetsk, and tried to storm Azovstal plant, inciting clashes with Ukrainian forces, including Azov Battalion, forces of 36th separate brigade of marines, units of the Military Forces, the National Guard, Border Guards and policemen. Casualties unknown.
On 21 May 2022, Russian military forcibly displaced 313 people from Mariupol, Donetsk, to filtration camps in Bezimenne, Donetsk.
Around 27 May 2022 (as reported), Russian shelling killed about 70 civilians in Mariupol, Donetsk.
On 3 June 2022, two civilians were killed by an unidentified explosive device in Mariupol, Donetsk.
On 10 June 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled DPR positions in Vodyane, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.
On 11 June 2022, a tractor exploded on a Russian mine in Mariupol, Donetsk. 1 Russian soldier and 2 officers of the Ministry of Emergency Situations were killed.
On 21 June 2022, two Russian soldiers died in mine explosions as they were demining the Azovstal plant in Mariupol, Donetsk.
On 3 July 2022, a Russian landing craft exploded on an unidentified mine near Mariupol, Donetsk. At least 3 Russian sailors were killed.
On 9 July 2022, 3 Russian sappers hit an unidentified mine in Mariupol, Donetsk while demining a gas pipeline. 3 Russian sappers were killed.

Around 15 July 2022 (month of), Russia commanded civilians to assist in sorting debris out in Mariupol, Donetsk. In a course of the sorting, 8 civilians were killed when a previously unexploded ordnance or IEDs went off.
Weapons seizure: On 8 August 2022, Russian forces located and seized a large Ukrainian cache of weapon and ammunition on the territory of Ilyich's metallurgical plant in Mariupol, Donetsk.

On 14 August 2022, Russian military forces fired artillery near Vodyane, Donetsk. According to Russian sources, the Russian military hit Ukrainian forces with a precise weapon, killing up to 260 soldiers near Vuhledar, Dobropillia and Vodyane. [Russian MoD reported 260 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event].

Around 18 August 2022 (as reported), Russian forces commanded civilians to assist in sorting debris out in Mariupol, Donetsk. In a course of the sorting, 2 civilians were killed when a previously unexploded ordnance or IEDs went off.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 21 August 2022, Russian air forces carried out air strikes near Vodyane, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

Defusal: On 15 September 2022, explosions, likely as a result of controlled detonation, were heard from on two plants in Mariupol, Donetsk. The demining in Mariupol was conducted by Russian military forces and civilians.
On 5 October 2022, a civilian was injured in Mariupol, Donetsk region, when an explosive, likely a Russian landmine or munition, went off.

On 10 October 2022, Russian forces shelled near Nikolske, Donetsk. Russia claims that the Ukrainian losses in this direction included one tank and three armored fighting vehicles. Russia claims that more than 30 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in Novomaiorske, Stepove and Nikolske. [Russian MoD reported 30 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event]

events.actor2: Military Forces of Ukraine (2019-) Air Force

On 23 December 2022, Russian forces shelled, assaulted, and were repulsed from Vodyane, Mariupolskyi district, Donetsk. Russian sources claim to have killed 40 Ukrainian soldiers. [Russian MoD reported 40 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities]
Security measures: Around 28 December 2022 (week of), Russian forces imposed a ban on any mass gatherings in the temporarily occupied Mariupol, Donetsk region, for the New Year celebration period.

On 20 January 2023, Ukrainian partisans planted an IED explosive on the car of a Russian military official in Mariupol, Donetsk region. As a result of the explosion, 3 military officials and the driver died.

On 13 May 2023, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian forces near Respublika, Donetsk. Around 50 Russian servicemen were killed.
On 22 May 2023, explosions (presumably due to a Ukrainian missile strike) were heard in Mariupol, Donetsk. Casualties unknown.

On 26 May 2023, Ukrainian forces shelled Russian forces in Mariupol, Donetsk region, near the Azovstal factory. Russian sources claimed that Russian air defenses shot down one of the two missiles. Ukrainian sources claim 250 Russian soldiers were killed in the shelling.
On 28 May 2023, Ukrainian forces shelled a Russian concentration area in the vicinity of Mariupol, Donetsk region, killing 100 Russian servicemen and wounding 40.
On 30 May 2023, Ukrainian forces shelled the base of Russian forces near Mariupol, Donetsk. Estimate casualties amount to around 100 killed and 400 injured Russian soldiers.

According to Russian sources, more than 75 Ukrainian soldiers were killed near Shevchenko, Nikolske and Novomykhaylivka [Russian MoD reported 75 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event].

According to Russian sources, more than 75 Ukrainian soldiers were killed near Shevchenko, Nikolske and Novomykhaylivka [Russian MoD reported 75 fatalities. Around 27 November 2023 (week of), a suspected partisan group killed by stabbing four Russian soldiers in Mariupol, Donetsk. Their bodies were found on the beach next to a picture of Russian President Putin with the Ukrainian word 'leave' written in the blood of one of the men on it.

events.notes: On 2 October 2021, Kharkiv residents staged a performance in protest against strict regulations in a local park in Kharkiv, banning visitors to sit on grass and street musicians to perform there.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 1 March 2022, in the morning, Russian air force and Russian military fired Grad missiles at the 3rd hospital, killing 8 people and wounding 6. Russian forces also fired at houses on Novobavarskyi boulevard, killing 5 people and wounding around 10, and at the Botanical Garden metro station in Kharkiv. Missile attack at the central square killed 10 people and wounded 35. In total, over 50 people were wounded and 29, including three children and one Indian student, were killed. 87 houses were ruined, including the consulate of Slovenia.

On 8 March 2022, Ukrainian forces clashed with 120 Russian paratroopers arriving on 3 helicopters in the Vovchansk district of the Kharkiv region. Most of the Russian group was eliminated, according to Ukrainian officials. Around 70 Russian soldiers were killed.
On 9 March 2022, Russian forces shelled a residential building in Izium, Kharkiv region, as a result of which at least 44 civilians were killed.
On 13 March 2022, Russian forces shelled residential areas of Izium, Kharkiv, with artillery and mortars. On 10 May 2022, about 44 corpses of civilians were found under the rubble.
On 21 March 2022, Ukrainian artillery forces hit Russian military targets in Tsyrkuny village, Kharkiv oblast. Casualties unknown.

On 25 March 2022, Ukrainian forces clashed with Russian forces near Kharkiv, taking control of an electronic warfare system truck and killing around 50 Russian soldiers working around it. 17 soldiers were taken prisoners, 15 of which were from NAF forces.

On 31 March 2022, Ukrainian forces shelled and fully destroyed the 237th tank regiment of the 3rd motorized infantry division near Izium, Kharkiv as a result of which 31 Russian servicemen were killed and 33 wounded.

events.actor1: Police Forces of Russia (2000-) Federal Security Service
events.notes: Around 15 April 2022 (month of), the Russian Federal Security Services stopped a civilian car with a local resident in Vovchansk, Kharkiv. They accused him of collaborating with the Ukrainian military and drove him to the local prosecutor's office, where the Russians had set up a torture chamber. They forcibly dragged the man into the basement, where, in addition to him, there were already more than 10 illegally detained people. They tied wires to his earlobes and electrocuted him with an electric shock device, and also filed his teeth with a file. After a while, the civilian man managed to escape by swimming across the river and reached the territory controlled by Ukraine.

On 22 April 2022, Russian forces tried to advance near Virnopillia , Kharkiv, clashing with Ukrainian forces. Ukrainians destroyed three tanks and three vehicles together with the personnel. Some 50 Russian soldiers were killed.
On 2 May 2022, a Russian armored unit engaged a Ukrainian mechanized unit near Zavody, Kharkiv during which at least one Ukrainian IFV was destroyed. Casualties unknown.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 6 May 2022, Russian air forces struck the Ukrainian military at the railway station near Bohodukhiv, Kharkiv. In Skovorodyvka, Dachne, Bohodukhiv, and across 15 other unspecified locations, Russian forces killed 280 Ukrainian soldiers (fatalities split across 3 known locations and coded as 93).

Around 15 July 2022 (month of), during the Russian occupation of Bayrak, Russian forces captured a woman and her 3 children in Bayrak, Kharkiv region. They were kept in the basement of their house and the woman was physically abused. Currently, their whereabouts are unknown.

On 18 July 2022, Ukrainian forces destroyed a diversionary reconnaissance group of the Russian Northern Fleet Marines in Dementiivka, Kharkiv, killing 36 Russian soldiers. Many more Russian servicemen received wounds of varying degrees of severity.
On 23 July 2022, Russian forces fired artillery near Zolochiv, Kharkiv. 1 civilian was killed and 4 civilians were injured.

On 31 December 2024, Russian Forces shelled Olhivske, Zaporizhia, with artillery. No casualties.

events.actor1: Military Forces of Russia (2000-) Air Force
events.notes: On 31 December 2024, Russian Forces launched air strikes at Stepnohirsk, Zaporizhia. According to Russian sources, 40 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in the area of Stepnohirsk, Prydniprovske and Beryslav [Russian MoD reported 40 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 3 events. 4 fatalities coded to this event].

On 31 December 2024, Russian Forces clashed with Ukrainian position near Bohatyr, Zaporizhia. According to Russian sources, 150 Ukrainian soldiers were killed in the area of Rozlyv, Velyka Novosilka, Neskuchne, Zelene Pole and Bohatyr [Russian MoD reported 150 fatalities. Coded as 10 fatalities split across 5 events. 2 fatalities coded to this event].
On 31 December 2024, Russian Forces shelled Malynivka, Zaporizhia, with artillery. No casualties.
